cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. So the task is to determine which sentence is NOT true based on the provided passage about Bobby Moresco. The options are A, B, C, and D. I need to check each one against the information given in the text. First, let me recall the details from the passage. Bobby grew up in Hell's Kitchen, a working-class neighborhood on Manhattan's West Side. Hell's Kitchen is next to Broadway. He was interested in acting from a young age but kept it secret because his peers might mock him. He took acting lessons at 17 but struggled, making only $2000 in the 70s. Later, he moved to Hollywood, drove a cab and worked as a waiter. His father told him to stop the "craziness" and get a job, as he had a wife and daughter. After his brother's murder in 1983, he moved back and started writing, leading to "Half-Deserted Streets" about his brother's death. The play led to screenplay work. He faced more struggles until 2005 when "Crash" won awards, making him a success at 54. Now, let's check each option: Option A: "His father did not support his work as a bartender." Wait, in the passage, it says his father told him to stop the "craziness" and get a job because he had a wife and daughter. The text mentions he worked as a cab driver and waiter, not a bartender. So maybe there's a mistake here. The father didn't support his acting career and his jobs in Hollywood were as a cab driver and waiter, not a bartender. The option says "bartender," which isn't mentioned. So A might be the incorrect statement. Option B: "Before he became an overnight success, his life experienced ups and downs." The passage describes his struggles in the 70s, moving to Hollywood, financial difficulties, brother's death, and then eventually success with Crash. So B is true.
